message.content:
You are resuming a crashed agent session on the bim-ai repo
  (/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
  tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest).

  REPO ORIENTATION (WP-D relevant paths):
  - packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx — project browser panel
  - packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx — browser tests
  - packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx — plan view canvas
  - packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ts — element → 2D geometry
  - packages/web/src/plan/PlanViewHeader.tsx — plan header bar
  - packages/web/src/plan/ViewRangeDialog.tsx — view range dialog (DONE)
  - packages/web/src/plan/viewRange.test.ts — view range tests (DONE)
  - packages/web/src/plan/ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx — RCP header test stub (exists)
  - packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts — RCP projection test stub (exists)
  - packages/web/src/plan/CalloutMarker.tsx — callout marker
  - packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionTool.tsx — detail region placement
  - packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionRenderer.tsx — detail region rendering
  - packages/web/src/plan/detailCallout.test.ts — detail callout test stub (UNTRACKED)
  - packages/web/src/plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx — color fill scheme dialog (exists, verify completeness)
  - packages/web/src/plan/colorFillLegend.ts — color fill legend (exists)
  - packages/web/src/workspace/sheets/SheetCanvas.tsx — sheet canvas
  - packages/web/src/OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx — orbit viewpoint persistence UI
  - packages/core/src/index.ts — Element types

  Architecture:
  - Views are model elements with a kind discriminator ('plan_view', 'section_view', etc.).
  - The workspace tabsModel.ts controls which views are open/active.
  - Semantic commands: { type: 'createView', ... }, { type: 'updateViewCrop', ... }.
  - Plan projection: planProjection.ts produces 2D geometry from model elements + view settings. Study this before touching RCP.
  - Tests: co-located *.test.ts, run with pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web.
  - Prettier runs automatically.

  WHAT WAS DONE BEFORE THE CRASH:
  - D3 View Range Dialog: DONE — plan/ViewRangeDialog.tsx + viewRange.test.ts, resolveViewRange helper, isAboveCutPlane, viewDepth field
  - D7 Project Browser (partial): UNSTAGED — ProjectBrowser.tsx + ProjectBrowser.test.tsx have ceiling plan section added but NOT committed
  - D8 Color Fill Scheme: PARTIAL — ColorSchemeDialog.tsx + colorFillLegend.ts exist; verify whether dispatch and rendering are fully wired

  Test stubs exist but not yet integrated: ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx, planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts, detailCallout.test.ts (untracked).

  STEP 0 — COMMIT UNSTAGED WORK FIRST:
    git pull --rebase origin main
    pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- ProjectBrowser
    git add packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx \
            packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx
    git commit -m "feat(views): add Deckenansichten section to project browser (D7 partial)"
    git push origin main

  Then read plan/detailCallout.test.ts (untracked) and plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx before starting new work.

  WHAT STILL NEEDS TO BE DONE:

  D7 — Finish Project Browser tree enhancements
  The ceiling plan section was just committed. Complete the rest:
  - Renaming: double-click a view name in the browser to edit it inline. Dispatch { type: 'renameView', viewId, newName }.
  - Context menu on each view: Duplicate, Delete, Properties. The onPropertiesView prop was just added — wire it to open the view's inspector.
  - Active view highlighting: currently open view should be visually highlighted.
  - Groups subtree: placeholder for when WP-B lands (leave a TODO comment in the right place).

  D1 — Reflected Ceiling Plan (RCP) view type
  Test stubs already exist in ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx and planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts. Implement to make them pass:
  1. Add 'ceiling_plan' as a planViewSubtype value (or a separate view kind)
  2. In planProjection.ts when viewType === 'ceiling_plan':
     - X-axis is mirrored (left/right flip)
     - Cut plane reads from topOfLevel − ceilingCutHeight (default 100mm below ceiling)
     - Visible: ceilings, light fixtures, structural beams at ceiling height
     - Hidden: floors, terrain, standard furniture
  3. View creation menu: add "Reflected Ceiling Plan" option; auto-create one per level
  4. Plan header label: "RCP - Ebene 0" in PlanViewHeader.tsx

  D2 — Interior Elevation Placement (4-Direction Marker, not started)
  Extend the 'elevation' tool with "Interior" mode via options bar:
  1. Grammar: click inside room → 4-directional marker appears (all 4 arrows enabled) → each arrow toggleable → Enter confirms
  2. Creates: 1 interior_elevation_marker element + up to 4 elevation_view elements (one per enabled direction) with viewingDirection: 0|90|180|270, auto-computed fovMm
  3. Plan: square with 4 outward arrows — filled = enabled, hollow = disabled
  4. Elevation views listed under "Schnitte/Ansichten" in project browser
  Tests: place marker in room, verify 4 views created with correct viewing directions

  D4 — Detail Callout enlarged view workflow
  CalloutMarker.tsx and DetailRegionTool.tsx exist. Read detailCallout.test.ts (untracked) first to understand what's expected. Complete:
  1. When callout placed (DetailRegionTool), immediately create a detail_view element: { kind: 'detail_view', sourceCalloutId, cropMinMm, cropMaxMm, scaleFactor: 5 }
  2. The detail view opens as a new workspace tab
  3. Elements render at the larger scale
  4. In parent plan: callout bubble shows sheet/view reference ("See 1/A-201")
  5. Project browser: detail view listed as child of its parent plan view
  Commit detailCallout.test.ts as part of this sub-task after making it pass.

  D5 — Named Locked 3D Views
  OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx already saves named viewpoints. Extend:
  1. "Save 3D View As…" saves a saved_3d_view element with camera matrix + section box state
  2. Saved views in project browser under "3D-Ansichten" (open, duplicate, rename, delete)
  3. Placeable on a sheet as a viewport
  4. Lock toggle: padlock icon in 3D view header prevents accidental orbit

  D6 — Sheet Revision Management (not started)
  1. Element types in core/index.ts: revision, sheet_revision, revision_cloud
  2. "Manage Revisions" button in sheet properties → table (add/edit/delete)
  3. "Revisions on Sheet" in sheet inspector
  4. Revision table rendered in sheet title block (Rev#, Date, Description)
  5. 'revision-cloud' ToolId: draw closed boundary, renders as scalloped curve
  Tests: create revision, apply to sheet, verify table renders

  D8 — Verify/complete Color Fill Scheme Dialog
  ColorSchemeDialog.tsx and colorFillLegend.ts exist. Read them:
  - If fully wired (dispatches applyColorScheme, rooms fill at 30% opacity, legend renders), mark as Done in tracker.
  - If something is missing, complete it.
  Tests: 3 rooms with "By Name" scheme → verify each room polygon has correct fill color.

  RULES:
  - git pull --rebase origin main before every major sub-task
  - Commit + push after each completed sub-task
  - Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation tools, export pipeline, phase dialogs, structural, or massing files
  - toolRegistry.ts is shared — always rebase before editing
  - pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web before each push
  - Update spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md as you complete items

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

  select: contract(
    'select',
    'review',
    ['plan', '3d', 'section', 'sheet', 'schedule'],
    'Selection cursor with hover hit feedback and no model mutation.',
    'selection-only',
  ),
  query: contract(
    'query',
    'review',
    ['plan'],
    'Hover/click imported underlay geometry and report the source primitive.',
    'stay-active',
    ['visible-import-underlay'],
  ),
  wall: contract(
    'wall',
    'line',
    ['plan', '3d'],
    'Two-click wall center/location line preview or picked floor/CAD line preview; committed payload must match preview endpoints.',
    'remain-active-while-looping',
    [],
    ['type', 'level', 'height', 'location-line', 'chain', 'flip', 'pick-lines'],
  ),
  door: contract(
    'door',
    'hosted',
    ['plan', '3d'],

input.description: Check WP-D status in parity tracker

content.content:
256:#### 2.4.4 Prioritäten (material layer priority for wall/floor/ceiling joins)
264:Shaft openings exist as a tool (shaft in tool registry). Stair side wall preparation workflow (drawing a shaft/void through floors and ceilings) is partially supported.
282:Walls have top constraint (connects to level above) and base offset in properties. Not all elements expose full level-relative constraint properties. Floor, ceiling, and roof level constraints are partially implemented.
598:#### 6.1.2 Die Deckenpläne (reflected ceiling plan views)
600:Reflected ceiling plans (RCP) — a view looking upward showing ceiling structure — are not explicitly implemented as a separate view type. The ceiling tool exists but a dedicated RCP view mode is Not Started.
709:### 8.2 Decken und Lampen (ceilings and light fixtures)
711:Ceiling tool is in the tool registry. Ceiling with automatic boundary from enclosing walls is Partial. Placing light fixtures (MEP-terminal/fixture) on ceilings works. Ceiling with grid pattern overlay in plan view is Not Started.
994:#### 13.1.3 Farbenlegenden (color fill legend: rooms colored by department, area, etc.)
996:roomSchemeColor.ts, roomColorSchemeLegendReadout.ts, roomFinishScheduleEvidenceReadout.ts exist. Color schemes are partially implemented. A user-facing color fill dialog (pick scheme category, set colors per value) is Partial.
1109:| 6 | Views & Sheets | Partial | RCP, interior elevs, plot, detail views, revisions |
1139:- Reflected ceiling plan view type (Ch. 6.1.2)
1144:- Room color fill scheme dialog (Ch. 13.1.3)

export function getToolRegistry(t: TFunction): Record<ToolId, ToolDefinition> {
  return {
    select: {
      id: 'select',
      label: t('tools.select.label'),
      icon: 'select',
      hotkey: 'V',
      shortcut: 'V',
      modes: ['plan', '3d', 'section', 'sheet', 'schedule'],
      tooltip: t('tools.select.tooltip'),
    },
    query: {
      id: 'query',
      label: 'Query',
      icon: 'search',
      hotkey: 'Q',
      shortcut: 'Q',
      modes: ['plan'],
      tooltip: 'Inspect imported CAD layer under the pointer',
    },
    wall: {
      id: 'wall',
      label: t('tools.wall.label'),
      icon: 'wall',
      hotkey: 'W',
      shortcut: 'W',
      modes: ['plan', '3d'],
      tooltip: t('tools.wall.tooltip'),
    },
    door: {
      id: 'door',
      label: t('tools.door.label'),
      icon: 'door',
      hotkey: 'D',
      shortcut: 'D',
      modes: ['plan', '3d'],
      tooltip: t('tools.door.tooltip'),
    },
    window: {
      id: 'window',
      label: t('tools.window.label'),
      icon: 'window',
      hotkey: 'Shift+W',
      shortcut: 'WI',
      modes: ['plan', '3d'],
      tooltip: t('tools.window.tooltip'),
    },
    floor: {
      id: 'floor',
      label: t('tools.floor.label'),
      icon: 'floor',
      hotkey: 'F',
      shortcut: 'F',
      modes: ['plan', '3d'],
      tooltip: t('tools.floor.tooltip'),
    },
    'floor-sketch': {
      id: 'floor-sketch',
      label: 'Floor (Sketch)',
      icon: 'floor',
      hotkey: 'Shift+F',
      modes: ['plan'],
      tooltip: 'Author a floor by drawing its boundary loop (Revit-style sketch mode).',
    },
    'roof-sketch': {
      id: 'roof-sketch',
      label: 'Roof (Sketch)',
      icon: 'roof',
      hotkey: 'Shift+O',

122:bim-ai has a workspace/project browser concept. Plan views, 3D views, section views, sheet views are accessible via tabs/navigation. Missing:
188:LevelStack.tsx, level datums in 3D view, level-based authoring all implemented. Levels can be created, renamed, and assigned elevations.
212:Levels can be positioned at any elevation. The 3D view shows level datum lines. Interactively dragging level heights works via grips.
214:#### 2.1.9 Die 3D-Ansicht (default 3D view, orbit, home view)
216:Three.js 3D viewport, orbit/pan/zoom, ViewCube, home view restore — all implemented.
337:### 3.2 3D-Ansicht für ein Geschoss über View Cube (orienting 3D view via ViewCube)
514:`spot-elevation` ToolId (hotkey `SE`) added. Single-click grammar. Plan renderer draws elevation label (prefix+mm/1000+suffix). Grip provider (position drag) and inspector panel (elevationMm editable) added. 3D viewport text label Not Started.
606:#### 6.1.2 Die Deckenpläne (reflected ceiling plan views)
608:Reflected ceiling plans (RCP) — a view looking upward showing ceiling structure — are not explicitly implemented as a separate view type. The ceiling tool exists but a dedicated RCP view mode is Not Started.
610:#### 6.1.3 3D-Ansichten (3D views: orthographic, perspective, section box, locked views)
615:- Locked 3D view (saved camera position that can be placed on a sheet): Partial — orbit viewpoint persistence exists (OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx) but named locked 3D views in the project browser are Partial
621:#### 6.1.5 Innenansichten (interior elevation views)
633:### 6.3 Plan mit Änderungsliste (sheet with revision table / delta list)
639:#### 6.4.1 Detailausschnitt (detail callout / enlarged plan area)
641:CalloutMarker.tsx and DetailRegionTool.tsx / DetailRegionRenderer.tsx exist. Placed detail callout regions appear in plan. The corresponding enlarged detail view viewport is Partial.
1002:#### 13.1.3 Farbenlegenden (color fill legend: rooms colored by department, area, etc.)
1004:roomSchemeColor.ts, roomColorSchemeLegendReadout.ts, roomFinishScheduleEvidenceReadout.ts exist. Color schemes are partially implemented. A user-facing color fill dialog (pick scheme category, set colors per value) is Partial.
1037:#### 14.2.1 Statische Anzeige (static shadow display in 3D view)
1039:SunOverlay.tsx, sunStore.ts, sunPositionNoaa.ts — static shadow display in 3D view with date/time/location is implemented.
1117:| 6 | Views & Sheets | Partial | RCP, interior elevs, plot, detail views, revisions |
1152:- Room color fill scheme dialog (Ch. 13.1.3)
1160:- Sheet revision management (Ch. 6.3)

message.content: This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: The user resumed a crashed agent session on the bim-ai repo to continue implementing WP-D (Views & Sheets) features for a browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest). The prompt specified: **STEP 0 (already done before crash)**: Commit unstaged ProjectBrowser ceiling plan section, run tests, push. **Remaining WP-D Tasks**: - D7: Complete ProjectBrowser tree (inline rename, context menu, active view highlight, groups TODO) - D1: Reflected Ceiling Plan (RCP) view type — test stubs exist - D2: Interior Elevation Placement (4-direction marker) - D4: Detail Callout enlarged view workflow - D5: Named Locked 3D Views - D6: Sheet Revision Management - D8: Verify/complete Color Fill Scheme Dialog **Rules**: git pull --rebase before major sub-tasks, commit + push after each, don't touch stair/ramp/annotation/export/phase/structural/massing files, always rebase before editing toolRegistry.ts. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - bim-ai: browser-based BIM authoring tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest) - Views as model elements with `kind` discriminators ('plan_view', 'section_view', etc.) - `ToolId` TypeScript union type in `toolRegistry.ts` — must match `AUTHORING_COMMAND_CONTRACTS` keys - `AUTHORING_COMMAND_CONTRACTS satisfies Record` — TypeScript satisfies for type safety but esbuild strips at test time - `buildToolCapabilities()` in `commandCapabilities.ts` iterates all tools and calls `getAuthoringCommandContract(tool.id)` - Command palette registry (`registerCommand`) must match capability graph entries - Reflected Ceiling Plan (RCP): `planViewSubtype === 'ceiling_plan'`, mirrors X-axis, different hidden kinds - `resolvePlanViewDisplay` in `planProjection.ts` returns `isRcp`, `hiddenSemanticKinds` - `tabFromElement` in `tabsModel.ts` maps elements to workspace tabs - Prettier runs automatically via PostToolUse hook after every Edit/Write - Multiple agents can work concurrently — commits from other agents may appear mid-session - Pre-existing failures: ProjectSetupDialog (Leaflet), jspdf module not installed, ToolPalette ("Element type is invalid") 3. addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

content.content:
256:#### 2.4.4 Prioritäten (material layer priority for wall/floor/ceiling joins)
264:Shaft openings exist as a tool (shaft in tool registry). Stair side wall preparation workflow (drawing a shaft/void through floors and ceilings) is partially supported.
282:Walls have top constraint (connects to level above) and base offset in properties. Not all elements expose full level-relative constraint properties. Floor, ceiling, and roof level constraints are partially implemented.
302:- Phase graphic overrides do not yet apply to roof, ceiling, railing, beam, stair elements
606:#### 6.1.2 Die Deckenpläne (reflected ceiling plan views)
608:Reflected ceiling plans (RCP) — a view looking upward showing ceiling structure — are not explicitly implemented as a separate view type. The ceiling tool exists but a dedicated RCP view mode is Not Started.
621:#### 6.1.5 Innenansichten (interior elevation views)
639:#### 6.4.1 Detailausschnitt (detail callout / enlarged plan area)
641:CalloutMarker.tsx and DetailRegionTool.tsx / DetailRegionRenderer.tsx exist. Placed detail callout regions appear in plan. The corresponding enlarged detail view viewport is Partial.
717:### 8.2 Decken und Lampen (ceilings and light fixtures)
719:Ceiling tool is in the tool registry. Ceiling with automatic boundary from enclosing walls is Partial. Placing light fixtures (MEP-terminal/fixture) on ceilings works. Ceiling with grid pattern overlay in plan view is Not Started.
1002:#### 13.1.3 Farbenlegenden (color fill legend: rooms colored by department, area, etc.)
1004:roomSchemeColor.ts, roomColorSchemeLegendReadout.ts, roomFinishScheduleEvidenceReadout.ts exist. Color schemes are partially implemented. A user-facing color fill dialog (pick scheme category, set colors per value) is Partial.
1147:- Reflected ceiling plan view type (Ch. 6.1.2)
1152:- Room color fill scheme dialog (Ch. 13.1.3)

content.content:
604	Plan views per level, crop region, plan detail level — all implemented.
605	
606	#### 6.1.2 Die Deckenpläne (reflected ceiling plan views)
607	**Status: Partial — P1**
608	Reflected ceiling plans (RCP) — a view looking upward showing ceiling structure — are not explicitly implemented as a separate view type. The ceiling tool exists but a dedicated RCP view mode is Not Started.
609	
610	#### 6.1.3 3D-Ansichten (3D views: orthographic, perspective, section box, locked views)
611	**Status: Partial — P1**
612	- Standard 3D orthographic/perspective: Done
613	- ViewCube navigation: Done (Partial parity — see Ch. 3)
614	- Section box: Done (sectionBox.ts)
615	- Locked 3D view (saved camera position that can be placed on a sheet): Partial — orbit viewpoint persistence exists (OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx) but named locked 3D views in the project browser are Partial
616	
617	#### 6.1.4 Außenansichten (elevation views: North, South, East, West)
618	**Status: Partial — P1**
619	Elevation tool and elevation marker exist. Four cardinal elevation views are auto-created with a new project in Revit. In bim-ai, elevation views must be placed manually. Elevation view rendering from the model (showing actual geometry in 2D elevation projection) is in sectionViewportSvg.tsx/sectionViewportDoc.ts — Partial.
620	
621	#### 6.1.5 Innenansichten (interior elevation views)
622	**Status: Partial — P2**
623	Interior elevation placement workflow (Revit: place 4-sided elevation marker inside a room, get 4 elevation views automatically) is Not Started. Elevation tool places single elevation views.
624	
625	#### 6.1.6 Schnittansicht (section view: cross section, building section)
626	**Status: Partial — P1**
627	Section tool exists, section views are generated (sectionViewportSvg.tsx). A fully rendered and annotated building section view matching Revit quality (with automatic material hatch patterns, cut line weights, section head bubbles) is Partial.
628	
629	### 6.2 Planerstellung (sheet setup: sheet with title block)
630	**Status: Partial — P1**
631	NewSheetDialog.tsx, SheetCanvas.tsx, SheetReviewSurface.tsx exist. Sheets can be created and views placed on them. Missing: user-customisable title blocks (Schriftkopf) with project information fields, dynamic title block families, viewport scale labels on sheets.
632	
633	### 6.3 Plan mit Änderungsliste (sheet with revision table / delta list)
634	**Status: Not Started — P2**
635	Revision tracking per sheet (Revit's Revision cloud + Revision schedule in title block) is not implemented.
636	
637	### 6.4 Detailansichten und Detaillierung (detail views and 2D detailing)
638	
639	#### 6.4.1 Detailausschnitt (detail callout / enlarged plan area)
640	**Status: Partial — P2**
641	CalloutMarker.tsx and DetailRegionTool.tsx / DetailRegionRenderer.tsx exist. Placed detail callout regions appear in plan. The corresponding enlarged detail view viewport is Partial.
642	
643	#### 6.4.2 Detailansicht (detail view: 2D drawing in isolation)
644	**Status: Partial — P2**
645	detailComponentsRender.ts exists. 2D detail components (insulation, section hatching, fill patterns) are Partial. A full Revit-style 2D detail view where the architect draws independently of the 3D model is Not Started.
646	
647	### 6.5 Plot (printing to plotter/printer)
648	**Status: Not Started — P1**
649	Print to physical printer or export as raster image directly from bim-ai is Not Started. PDF export (see 12.4.5) is the workaround.
650	
651	### 6.6 Übungsfragen
652	**Status: N/A**
653	
654	---
655	
656	## Chapter 7 — Konstruktionshilfen (drafting aids)
657	
658	### 7.1 Modelllinien (model lines as 3D construction geometry)
659	
660	#### 7.1.1 Beispiel für Hilfskonstruktion (construction line example)
661	**Status: Partial — P1**
662	Model lines as persistent 3D sketch geometry (not just reference planes) are used in the family editor. In the project environment, model line as a general-purpose authoring aid is Partial.
663

file.content:
Plan views per level, crop region, plan detail level — all implemented.

#### 6.1.2 Die Deckenpläne (reflected ceiling plan views)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Reflected ceiling plans (RCP) — a view looking upward showing ceiling structure — are not explicitly implemented as a separate view type. The ceiling tool exists but a dedicated RCP view mode is Not Started.

#### 6.1.3 3D-Ansichten (3D views: orthographic, perspective, section box, locked views)
**Status: Partial — P1**
- Standard 3D orthographic/perspective: Done
- ViewCube navigation: Done (Partial parity — see Ch. 3)
- Section box: Done (sectionBox.ts)
- Locked 3D view (saved camera position that can be placed on a sheet): Partial — orbit viewpoint persistence exists (OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx) but named locked 3D views in the project browser are Partial

#### 6.1.4 Außenansichten (elevation views: North, South, East, West)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Elevation tool and elevation marker exist. Four cardinal elevation views are auto-created with a new project in Revit. In bim-ai, elevation views must be placed manually. Elevation view rendering from the model (showing actual geometry in 2D elevation projection) is in sectionViewportSvg.tsx/sectionViewportDoc.ts — Partial.

#### 6.1.5 Innenansichten (interior elevation views)
**Status: Partial — P2**
Interior elevation placement workflow (Revit: place 4-sided elevation marker inside a room, get 4 elevation views automatically) is Not Started. Elevation tool places single elevation views.

#### 6.1.6 Schnittansicht (section view: cross section, building section)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Section tool exists, section views are generated (sectionViewportSvg.tsx). A fully rendered and annotated building section view matching Revit quality (with automatic material hatch patterns, cut line weights, section head bubbles) is Partial.

### 6.2 Planerstellung (sheet setup: sheet with title block)
**Status: Partial — P1**
NewSheetDialog.tsx, SheetCanvas.tsx, SheetReviewSurface.tsx exist. Sheets can be created and views placed on them. Missing: user-customisable title blocks (Schriftkopf) with project information fields, dynamic title block families, viewport scale labels on sheets.

### 6.3 Plan mit Änderungsliste (sheet with revision table / delta list)
**Status: Not Started — P2**
Revision tracking per sheet (Revit's Revision cloud + Revision schedule in title block) is not implemented.

### 6.4 Detailansichten und Detaillierung (detail views and 2D detailing)

#### 6.4.1 Detailausschnitt (detail callout / enlarged plan area)
**Status: Partial — P2**
CalloutMarker.tsx and DetailRegionTool.tsx / DetailRegionRenderer.tsx exist. Placed detail callout regions appear in plan. The corresponding enlarged detail view viewport is Partial.

#### 6.4.2 Detailansicht (detail view: 2D drawing in isolation)
**Status: Partial — P2**
detailComponentsRender.ts exists. 2D detail components (insulation, section hatching, fill patterns) are Partial. A full Revit-style 2D detail view where the architect draws independently of the 3D model is Not Started.

### 6.5 Plot (printing to plotter/printer)
**Status: Not Started — P1**
Print to physical printer or export as raster image directly from bim-ai is Not Started. PDF export (see 12.4.5) is the workaround.

### 6.6 Übungsfragen
**Status: N/A**

---

## Chapter 7 — Konstruktionshilfen (drafting aids)

### 7.1 Modelllinien (model lines as 3D construction geometry)

#### 7.1.1 Beispiel für Hilfskonstruktion (construction line example)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Model lines as persistent 3D sketch geometry (not just reference planes) are used in the family editor. In the project environment, model line as a general-purpose authoring aid is Partial.

#### 6.1.2 Die Deckenpläne (reflected ceiling plan views)
**Status: Done — D1**
Reflected ceiling plans (RCP) are implemented as `planViewSubtype: 'ceiling_plan'`. `resolvePlanViewDisplay` in `planProjection.ts` sets `isRcp: true`, mirrors the X-axis, and adjusts `hiddenSemanticKinds` (floors/roofs hidden, ceilings/beams visible). `PlanViewHeader.tsx` shows the RCP badge. ProjectBrowser groups RCP views under "Deckenansichten". Tests: `ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx` (3 tests) + `planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts` (7 tests) all pass.

# WP-D Resume — Views, Sheets, RCP & Project Browser

You are resuming a crashed agent session on the **bim-ai** repo
(`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest). This prompt is
self-contained.

---

## Repo orientation (WP-D relevant paths)

```
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx         — project browser panel
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx    — browser tests
packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx                          — plan view canvas
packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ts                       — element → 2D geometry
packages/web/src/plan/PlanViewHeader.tsx                      — plan header bar
packages/web/src/plan/ViewRangeDialog.tsx                     — view range dialog (DONE)
packages/web/src/plan/viewRange.test.ts                       — view range tests (DONE)
packages/web/src/plan/ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx          — RCP header test (exists)
packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts      — RCP projection test (exists)
packages/web/src/plan/CalloutMarker.tsx                       — callout marker
packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionTool.tsx                    — detail region placement
packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionRenderer.tsx                — detail region rendering
packages/web/src/plan/detailCallout.test.ts                   — detail callout test (untracked stub)
packages/web/src/plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx                   — color fill scheme dialog (exists)
packages/web/src/plan/colorFillLegend.ts                      — color fill legend (exists)
packages/web/src/workspace/sheets/SheetCanvas.tsx             — sheet canvas
packages/web/src/OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx               — orbit viewpoint UI
packages/core/src/index.ts                                    — Element types
```

Architecture patterns:
- Views are model elements with a `kind` discriminator (`'plan_view'`,
  `'section_view'`, etc.) stored in the project model.
- The workspace `tabsModel.ts` controls which views are open/active.
- Semantic commands: `{ type: 'createView', ... }`, `{ type: 'updateViewCrop', ... }`.
- Plan projection: `planProjection.ts` produces 2D line geometry from model
  elements + view settings. Study this before touching RCP.
- Tests: co-located `*.test.ts`, run with `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
- Prettier runs automatically.

---

## What was done before the crash

| Sub-task | Status | Files |
|---|---|---|
| D3 View Range Dialog | **Done** | `plan/ViewRangeDialog.tsx` + `viewRange.test.ts`, `resolveViewRange` helper, `isAboveCutPlane`, `viewDepth` field |
| D7 Project Browser (partial) | **Partial — unstaged** | `ProjectBrowser.tsx` + `ProjectBrowser.test.tsx` have ceiling plan section added but NOT committed |
| D8 Color Fill Scheme | **Partial** | `plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx` + `colorFillLegend.ts` exist — verify completeness |

Test stubs created but not yet integrated: `plan/ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx`,
`plan/planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts`, `plan/detailCallout.test.ts`.

---

## Step 0 — commit the unstaged work FIRST

```bash
git pull --rebase origin main
pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- ProjectBrowser
git add packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx \
        packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx
git commit -m "feat(views): add Deckenansichten section to project browser (D7 partial)"
git push origin main
```

Then read `plan/detailCallout.test.ts` (untracked) and `plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx`
to understand their state before starting work.

---

## What still needs to be done

### D7 — Finish Project Browser tree enhancements

The ceiling plan section was added in the unstaged changes. Complete the rest:
- **Renaming**: double-click a view name in the browser to edit it inline.
  Dispatch `{ type: 'renameView', viewId, newName }`.
- **Context menu** on each view: Duplicate, Delete, Properties. The
  `onPropertiesView` prop was just added — wire it to open the view's inspector.
- **Active view highlighting**: the currently open view should be visually
  highlighted in the browser list.
- **Groups subtree**: once WP-B lands, add a "Gruppen" section listing group
  definitions + instance counts. For now, leave a TODO comment in the right
  place.

### D1 — Reflected Ceiling Plan (RCP) view type

Test stubs already exist. Implement to make them pass:

1. Add `'ceiling_plan'` as a `planViewSubtype` value (or a separate view kind).
2. In `planProjection.ts`: when `viewType === 'ceiling_plan'`:
   - X-axis is mirrored (left/right flip vs looking down)
   - Cut plane reads from `topOfLevel − ceilingCutHeight` (default 100mm below ceiling)
   - Visible: ceilings, light fixtures, structural beams at ceiling height
   - Hidden: floors, terrain, standard furniture
3. View creation menu: add "Reflected Ceiling Plan" option; auto-create one per level.
4. Plan header label: "RCP - Ebene 0" (update `PlanViewHeader.tsx`).
5. Make `ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx` and `planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts` pass.

### D2 — Interior Elevation Placement (4-Direction Marker)

Not started. Extend the `'elevation'` tool with an "Interior" mode via the
options bar:

1. Grammar (Interior mode):
   - Click inside a room → 4-directional marker appears, all 4 arrows enabled
   - Each arrow can be toggled on/off with a click
   - Enter confirms
2. Creates: 1 `interior_elevation_marker` element + up to 4 `elevation_view`
   elements (one per enabled direction), each with `viewingDirection: 0|90|180|270`,
   auto-computed `fovMm`, `cropMinMm/MaxMm`.
3. Plan rendering: square with 4 outward arrows — filled = enabled, hollow = disabled.
4. Elevation views listed under "Schnitte/Ansichten" in the project browser.

Tests: place marker in a room, verify 4 views created with correct viewing directions.

### D4 — Detail Callout enlarged view workflow

`CalloutMarker.tsx` and `DetailRegionTool.tsx` exist. `detailCallout.test.ts`
is an untracked test stub — read it first to understand what's expected.
Complete:

1. When a callout marker is placed (DetailRegionTool), immediately create a
   `detail_view` element: `{ kind: 'detail_view', sourceCalloutId, cropMinMm, cropMaxMm, scaleFactor: 5 }`
2. The detail view opens as a new workspace tab (same mechanism as plan views).
3. Elements render at the larger scale: thin lines, fill patterns visible.
4. In parent plan: callout bubble shows sheet/view reference ("See 1/A-201").
5. Project browser: detail view listed as child of its parent plan view.

Commit `detailCallout.test.ts` as part of this sub-task after making it pass.

### D5 — Named Locked 3D Views

`OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx` already saves named viewpoints. Extend:

1. "Save 3D View As…" prompts for a name and saves `saved_3d_view` element
   with camera matrix + section box state.
2. Saved views in the project browser under "3D-Ansichten" (can open, duplicate,
   rename, delete).
3. Placeable on a sheet as a viewport (same mechanism as plan views).
4. Lock toggle: padlock icon in the 3D view header prevents accidental orbit.

### D6 — Sheet Revision Management

Not started:

1. Element types in `core/index.ts`:
   - `revision`: `{ id, number, date, description, issuedBy, issuedTo }`
   - `sheet_revision`: `{ sheetId, revisionId }`
   - `revision_cloud`: `{ kind: 'revision_cloud', viewId, revisionId, boundaryPoints: XY[] }`
2. "Manage Revisions" button in sheet properties → table of revisions (add/edit/delete).
3. "Revisions on Sheet" in sheet inspector → add/remove applicable revisions.
4. Revision table in sheet title block (Rev#, Date, Description).
5. `'revision-cloud'` ToolId: draw closed boundary, renders as scalloped/bumpy curve.

Tests: create revision, apply to sheet, verify revision table renders.

### D8 — Verify / complete Color Fill Scheme Dialog

`plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx` and `plan/colorFillLegend.ts` exist. Read them:
- If fully wired (dispatch `applyColorScheme`, rooms filled at 30% opacity,
  legend element renders), mark as Done in the tracker.
- If there are missing pieces (e.g. the dialog exists but doesn't dispatch the
  command, or the legend element doesn't render in plan), complete them.

Tests: 3 rooms with "By Name" scheme → verify each room polygon has correct fill.

---

## Rules

- `git pull --rebase origin main` before every major sub-task
- Commit + push after each completed sub-task
- Do NOT touch stair/ramp, annotation tools, export pipeline, phase dialogs,
  structural, or massing files
- `toolRegistry.ts` is shared — always rebase before editing
- `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web` before each push
- Update `spec/revit-parity/revit2026-parity-tracker.md` as you complete items

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119	
120	#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
121	**Status: Partial — P1**
122	bim-ai has a workspace/project browser concept. Plan views, 3D views, section views, sheet views are accessible via tabs/navigation. Missing:

... [1043 lines truncated] ...

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94	
95	#### 1.6.6 Benutzung der Werkzeuge (how tools activate, options bar, modify ribbon)
96	**Status: Partial — P1**
97	bim-ai has a ToolPalette with hotkeys, OptionsBar, and ToolModifierBar. Revit-style "tool stays active until escape/finish" grammar is implemented. The chained-placement model (wall chain, stair by component, etc.) is done. Missing: the full floating options bar that changes per-tool is only partially implemented for some tools.
98	
99	#### 1.6.7 Exemplar- und Typeigenschaften, neue Elementtypen (instance vs type properties, type duplication)
100	**Status: Partial — P1**
101	bim-ai has the Inspector panel for element properties. Instance properties are shown and editable for walls, doors, windows, roofs, floors. Type properties and type duplication (WorkspaceRightRailTypeCommands.ts exists) are partially implemented. Creating entirely new types from scratch (e.g. new wall type with custom layer stack) is partially available in the wall type catalog. Full Revit-style "Typ bearbeiten" dialog with all type parameters is not yet fully parity.
102	
103	#### 1.6.8 Optionsleiste: wichtigste Exemplareigenschaften (options bar for active tool)
104	**Status: Partial — P1**
105	OptionsBar.tsx exists and is wired for wall placement (location line, chain mode, offset, radius). Not all tools have fully populated options bars as in Revit.
106	
107	#### 1.6.9 Statusleiste (status bar with command hints)
108	**Status: Partial — P2**
109	bim-ai shows contextual hints during active tool operations. Full Revit-style status bar with structured "Select element" / "Click to place" strings per tool state is not fully implemented.
110	
111	#### 1.6.10 Ansichtssteuerung (view controls: scale, detail level, visual style, shadows, crop)
112	**Status: Partial — P1**
113	bim-ai has:
114	- Plan detail level toolbar (PlanDetailLevelToolbar.tsx — Done)
115	- Visual style selection (renderStyles.ts — Done)
116	- Sun/shadow toggle (SunOverlay.tsx — Done)
117	- Scale display: Partial (shown in plan header)
118	Missing: per-view crop region editing, view-level visibility/graphics overrides dialog, thin-lines toggle ("Feine Linien"), hide/isolate element commands.
119	
120	#### 1.6.11 Projektbrowser (project browser tree: views, sheets, families, groups, Revit links)
121	**Status: Partial — P1**
122	bim-ai has a workspace/project browser concept. Plan views, 3D views, section views, sheet views are accessible via tabs/navigation. Missing:

... [1043 lines truncated] ...

content.text:
The memory index looks fine as-is. Here's a summary of what was accomplished in this session:

**D7 (Project Browser)**: Already fully implemented in `ProjectBrowserV3` — inline rename, context menu (Duplicate/Delete/Properties), active view highlighting, drag-to-reorder. Added Groups TODO comment. All tests pass.

**D1 (RCP)**: Already implemented from previous session. Updated tracker §6.1.2 from "Not Started" → "Done".

**D4 (Detail Callout)**: Already implemented. Updated tracker §6.4.1.

**D8 (Color Fill)**: Already implemented. Updated tracker §13.1.3.

**D5 (Named Locked 3D Views)**: `Save3dViewAsDialog.tsx` — a name-prompt modal before persisting `saved_view` elements, wired into `Workspace.tsx`. 7 tests pass.

**D6 (Sheet Revision Management)**: Added `revision` and `sheet_revision` element types + 5 CRUD commands to `@bim-ai/core`. `ManageRevisionsDialog.tsx` with full CRUD + per-sheet assignment via checkboxes. 11 tests pass.

Total: 3729 tests pass (up from ~3663 at session start), only the pre-existing Leaflet failure remains.

attachment.snippet:
1	# Revit 2026 Feature-Parity Tracker
2	
3	Last updated: 2026-05-16
4	Source: Detlef Ridder — *Autodesk Revit 2026: Der umfassende Praxiseinstieg für Architekturkonstruktion*, mitp 2026 (ISBN 978-3-7475-1101-5)
5	
6	Purpose: exhaustive chapter-by-chapter comparison between Revit 2026 (as taught in the book) and what bim-ai currently supports. Every leaf section of the table of contents becomes a row. The goal is to expose gaps at maximum granularity so engineering work can be scoped and prioritised.
7	
8	---
9	
10	## Status Legend
11	
12	- `Done` — fully implemented, tested, and working at production quality
13	- `Partial` — some sub-capability exists but parity is incomplete
14	- `Not Started` — no implementation exists
15	- `N/A` — not applicable to a browser-based BIM SaaS (installation flows, desktop-only licensing, Windows hardware checks, etc.)
16	
17	Priority:
18	- `P0` — core authoring workflow; blocks architects from meaningful use
19	- `P1` — important for professional parity; significantly limits daily use if absent
20	- `P2` — useful but architects can work around it today
21	- `P3` — niche, advanced, or rarely used in typical architectural projects
22	
23	---
24	
25	## Chapter 1 — Revit installieren, starten und loslegen (UI & startup)
26	
27	### 1.1 Die Testversion (trial licensing)
28	**Status: N/A**
29	Revit requires download and 30-day activation. bim-ai is browser-based with its own subscription/trial model. No parity gap.
30	
31	### 1.2 Die Studentenversion (student licensing)
32	**Status: N/A**
33	Autodesk-specific educational licensing. bim-ai has its own account system.
34	
35	### 1.3 Hard- und Software-Voraussetzungen (system requirements)
36	**Status: N/A**
37	Revit 2026 requires 64-bit Windows 10/11, 16 GB RAM, DirectX 11 GPU, 30 GB disk. bim-ai runs in a browser. No parity gap.
38	
39	### 1.4 Installierte Programme (installed components)
40	**Status: N/A**
41	Revit installs Revit main, Viewer, Add-Ins Manager, Worksharing Monitor. bim-ai is a web app.
42	
43	### 1.5 Revit starten (startup & project templates)
44	**Status: Partial — P2**
45	Revit shows a start screen with recently-used projects, a choice of project template (None, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau, BIM Architektur und Ingenieurbau vereinfacht, BIM Gebäudetechnik) and a new-project dialog. bim-ai has a project creation flow and project list, and now:
46	- Project templates (projectTemplates.ts) implemented: minimal, residential (Wohnbau), commercial (Gewerbebau) with level + phase setup commands. Template picker added to project creation dialog (ProjectSetupDialog.tsx, "Templates" section).
47	- Template picker allows selecting a template and applying its createLevel/createPhase commands in one click.
48	Still missing:
49	- No multi-template selection at project creation time (only one applied at a time)
50	- No "Revit 2026 start page" visual freshness (recently-used thumbnails, cloud projects)
51	- BIM Architektur vereinfacht template equivalent is missing
52	
53	### 1.6 Die Revit-Benutzeroberfläche (UI chrome)
54	
55	#### 1.6.1 Programmleiste (title bar with project + view name)
56	**Status: Partial — P2**
57	bim-ai shows current project name in the header. Current active view name is shown in the view tab. Full "Projekt1 - Grundriss: Ebene 0" combined title is not surfaced in a persistent title bar.
58	
59	#### 1.6.2 Dateimenü (file menu: New, Open, Save, Export, Print, Close)
60	**Status: Partial — P1**
61	bim-ai has:
62	- New project (Done)
63	- Open project (Done)
64	- Save / auto-save (Done)
65	- Export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
66	- Print / PDF export (Partial — see Ch. 12)
67	Missing: Save As template, Save to library as Family, cloud model sync from file menu, Revit Options dialog.
68	
69	#### 1.6.3 Schnellzugriff-Werkzeugkasten (quick access toolbar)
70	**Status: Partial — P2**
71	bim-ai has a fixed top bar with key actions (undo, redo, save, 3D toggle). Revit's QAT is fully user-customisable — any ribbon command can be pinned. bim-ai does not yet have a user-configurable quick access bar.
72	
73	#### 1.6.4 Die Info-Leiste (info bar: search help, Autodesk Account, App Store, Help)
74	**Status: Partial — P2**
75	bim-ai has a help/onboarding entry point but no in-product search over help documentation, no integrated App Store, no Autodesk Account management. The user profile/account menu exists.
76	
77	#### 1.6.5 Multifunktionsleiste, Register, Gruppen und Flyouts (ribbon with tabs, groups, flyouts)
78	**Status: Partial — P1**
79	Revit has a full ribbon with tabs: Architektur, Ingenieurbau, Stahlbau, Betonfertigteile, Gebäudetechnik, Einfügen, Beschriften, Berechnung, Körper & Grundstück, Zusammenarbeit, Ansicht, Verwalten, Zusatzmodule, Ändern. bim-ai has a compact vertical tool palette and a minimal top menu — no full ribbon architecture.
80	- Architecture tools (Architektur tab equivalent): Done for core tools; many sub-groups missing
81	- Structural tab (Ingenieurbau): Partial — columns and beams exist, no full structural ribbon
82	- Steel (Stahlbau): Not Started
83	- Precast (Betonfertigteile): Not Started
84	- MEP (Gebäudetechnik): Partial — duct/pipe/cable-tray/equipment in tool registry, no MEP-detail ribbon
85	- Insert (Einfügen): Partial — see Ch. 12
86	- Annotate (Beschriften): Partial — see Ch. 4
87	- Calculate (Berechnung): Not Started
88	- Mass & Site (Körper & Grundstück): Partial — see Ch. 11 & 5
89	- Collaborate (Zusammenarbeit): Partial — see collab features
90	- View (Ansicht): Partial
91	- Manage (Verwalten): Partial
92	- Add-ins (Zusatzmodule): N/A
93	- Modify (Ändern) context ribbon: Partial
94

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

# WP-D Resume — Views, Sheets, RCP & Project Browser

You are resuming a crashed agent session on the **bim-ai** repo
(`/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai`). bim-ai is a browser-based BIM authoring
tool (React + TypeScript + Three.js, Vite, Vitest). This prompt is
self-contained.

---

## Repo orientation (WP-D relevant paths)

```
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx         — project browser panel
packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx    — browser tests
packages/web/src/plan/PlanCanvas.tsx                          — plan view canvas
packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ts                       — element → 2D geometry
packages/web/src/plan/PlanViewHeader.tsx                      — plan header bar
packages/web/src/plan/ViewRangeDialog.tsx                     — view range dialog (DONE)
packages/web/src/plan/viewRange.test.ts                       — view range tests (DONE)
packages/web/src/plan/ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx          — RCP header test (exists)
packages/web/src/plan/planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts      — RCP projection test (exists)
packages/web/src/plan/CalloutMarker.tsx                       — callout marker
packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionTool.tsx                    — detail region placement
packages/web/src/plan/DetailRegionRenderer.tsx                — detail region rendering
packages/web/src/plan/detailCallout.test.ts                   — detail callout test (untracked stub)
packages/web/src/plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx                   — color fill scheme dialog (exists)
packages/web/src/plan/colorFillLegend.ts                      — color fill legend (exists)
packages/web/src/workspace/sheets/SheetCanvas.tsx             — sheet canvas
packages/web/src/OrbitViewpointPersistedHud.tsx               — orbit viewpoint UI
packages/core/src/index.ts                                    — Element types
```

Architecture patterns:
- Views are model elements with a `kind` discriminator (`'plan_view'`,
  `'section_view'`, etc.) stored in the project model.
- The workspace `tabsModel.ts` controls which views are open/active.
- Semantic commands: `{ type: 'createView', ... }`, `{ type: 'updateViewCrop', ... }`.
- Plan projection: `planProjection.ts` produces 2D line geometry from model
  elements + view settings. Study this before touching RCP.
- Tests: co-located `*.test.ts`, run with `pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web`.
- Prettier runs automatically.

---

## What was done before the crash

| Sub-task | Status | Files |
|---|---|---|
| D3 View Range Dialog | **Done** | `plan/ViewRangeDialog.tsx` + `viewRange.test.ts`, `resolveViewRange` helper, `isAboveCutPlane`, `viewDepth` field |
| D7 Project Browser (partial) | **Partial — unstaged** | `ProjectBrowser.tsx` + `ProjectBrowser.test.tsx` have ceiling plan section added but NOT committed |
| D8 Color Fill Scheme | **Partial** | `plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx` + `colorFillLegend.ts` exist — verify completeness |

Test stubs created but not yet integrated: `plan/ceilingPlanViewHeader.test.tsx`,
`plan/planProjection.ceilingPlan.test.ts`, `plan/detailCallout.test.ts`.

---

## Step 0 — commit the unstaged work FIRST

```bash
git pull --rebase origin main
pnpm test --filter @bim-ai/web -- ProjectBrowser
git add packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.tsx \
        packages/web/src/workspace/project/ProjectBrowser.test.tsx
git commit -m "feat(views): add Deckenansichten section to project browser (D7 partial)"
git push origin main
```

Then read `plan/detailCallout.test.ts` (untracked) and `plan/ColorSchemeDialog.tsx`
to understand their state before starting work.

---

## What still needs to be done

### D7 — Finish Project Browser tree enhancements

The ceiling plan section was added in the unstaged changes. Complete the rest:
- **Renaming**: double-click a view name in the browser to edit it inline.
  Dispatch `{ type: 'renameView', viewId, newName }`.

file.content:
**Status: Partial — P1**
Elevation tool and elevation marker exist. Four cardinal elevation views are auto-created with a new project in Revit. In bim-ai, elevation views must be placed manually. Elevation view rendering from the model (showing actual geometry in 2D elevation projection) is in sectionViewportSvg.tsx/sectionViewportDoc.ts — Partial.

#### 6.1.5 Innenansichten (interior elevation views)
**Status: Partial — P2**
Interior elevation placement workflow (Revit: place 4-sided elevation marker inside a room, get 4 elevation views automatically) is Not Started. Elevation tool places single elevation views.

#### 6.1.6 Schnittansicht (section view: cross section, building section)
**Status: Partial — P1**
Section tool exists, section views are generated (sectionViewportSvg.tsx). A fully rendered and annotated building section view matching Revit quality (with automatic material hatch patterns, cut line weights, section head bubbles) is Partial.

### 6.2 Planerstellung (sheet setup: sheet with title block)
**Status: Partial — P1**
NewSheetDialog.tsx, SheetCanvas.tsx, SheetReviewSurface.tsx exist. Sheets can be created and views placed on them. Missing: user-customisable title blocks (Schriftkopf) with project information fields, dynamic title block families, viewport scale labels on sheets.

`tool.interior-elevation` is already registered in `defaultCommands.ts`. The test failure says it's NOT in `paletteIds` — let me re-read the test failure:

```
→ expected [ 'tool.interior-elevation' ] to deeply equal []

Wait, the test says: capabilities that have `cmd-k` surface AND are not in the palette registry. `tool.interior-elevation` has `cmd-k` surface (because `buildToolCapabilities` sets `surfaces: ['ribbon', 'cmd-k']`), and the test found it NOT in the `paletteIds`.

But I can see in `defaultCommands.ts` line 796, it IS registered. Let me look more carefully — perhaps the test was run BEFORE my changes. Let me run it again:

1	import type { ToolId, WorkspaceMode } from './toolRegistry';
2	
3	export type AuthoringCommandKind =
4	  | 'line'
5	  | 'sketch'
6	  | 'hosted'
7	  | 'point'
8	  | 'modify'
9	  | 'review'
10	  | 'document'
11	  | 'resource';
12	
13	export type AuthoringCompletionBehavior =
14	  | 'select-after-commit'
15	  | 'remain-active-while-looping'
16	  | 'explicit-finish'
17	  | 'stay-active'
18	  | 'selection-only'
19	  | 'opens-resource';
20	
21	export interface AuthoringCommandContract {
22	  toolId: ToolId;
23	  kind: AuthoringCommandKind;
24	  validModes: WorkspaceMode[];
25	  requiredContext: string[];
26	  activeOptions: string[];
27	  previewSemantics: string;
28	  completionBehavior: AuthoringCompletionBehavior;
29	  defaultAfterCancel: 'select';
30	}
31	
32	function contract(
33	  toolId: ToolId,
34	  kind: AuthoringCommandKind,
35	  validModes: WorkspaceMode[],
36	  previewSemantics: string,
37	  completionBehavior: AuthoringCompletionBehavior,
38	  requiredContext: string[] = [],
39	  activeOptions: string[] = [],
40	): AuthoringCommandContract {
41	  return {
42	    toolId,
43	    kind,
44	    validModes,
45	    requiredContext,
46	    activeOptions,
47	    previewSemantics,
48	    completionBehavior,
49	    defaultAfterCancel: 'select',
50	  };
51	}
52
